coverageDefinitions.observed-in-mockups: Directly visible in at least one approved hi-fi mockup.
coverageDefinitions.partially-observed-in-mockups: Some visual or behavioral aspects appear in the mockups, but the component is not fully specified by them.
coverageDefinitions.prd-required: Required by product or workflow scope even if not fully visible in mockups.
coverageDefinitions.reference-backed-standard: Included because mature public references support it as a standard UI component or pattern.
coverageDefinitions.inferred-engineering-need: Included because engineering UIs commonly need it, but further project validation is required.
coverageDefinitions.reserved-for-future-validation: Reserved in the manifest so later phases can define it without ID churn.

categories.name: Shell
categories.purpose: Application frame and persistent global regions.

categories.name: Navigation
categories.purpose: Movement between app sections and workflow locations.

categories.name: Surface
categories.purpose: Containers, cards, panels, and visual grouping surfaces.

categories.name: Forms
categories.purpose: Inputs, labels, validation, and controlled data entry.

categories.name: Tables
categories.purpose: Tabular data, grids, rows, columns, pagination, and row actions.

categories.name: Filters
categories.purpose: Search, chip, and filtering controls for dense views.

categories.name: Feedback
categories.purpose: Alerts, toasts, validation summaries, and user-facing messages.

categories.name: Status
categories.purpose: Compact state indicators for jobs, results, and workflow state.

categories.name: Overlays
categories.purpose: Dialogs, drawers, popovers, menus, and tooltips.

categories.name: Workflow
categories.purpose: Multi-step, event, and execution workflow patterns.

categories.name: Engineering Artifacts
categories.purpose: Artifacts, evidence, requirements, traces, and test summaries.

categories.name: Code and Logs
categories.purpose: Code, logs, diffs, paths, and technical text.

categories.name: Content
categories.purpose: Text blocks, empty states, help content, and key-value summaries.

components.name: App Shell
components.description: a persistent application frame for dark-first engineering workflows.
useWhen: Use app shell when the interface needs a persistent application frame for dark-first engineering workflows.
avoidWhen: Avoid app shell when a simpler native element or existing component communicates the same intent with less complexity.

accessibility.keyboard: Keyboard access must match the native control or WAI-ARIA pattern for the rendered role.
accessibility.aria: Expose an accessible name, role, and state when the component is not a native semantic control.
accessibility.contrast: Text, icons, borders, and state indicators must meet WCAG contrast expectations for the dark theme.
accessibility.focus: Use visible focus styling based on semantic focus tokens and manage focus when opening or closing composite widgets.

implementationNotes: Use semantic token references only; do not hard-code visual values for app shell.
validation: Validate app shell for token usage, accessible names where applicable, dark-theme contrast, and visible focus states.

accessibility.keyboard: Keyboard access must match the native control or WAI-ARIA pattern for the rendered role.
accessibility.aria: Expose an accessible name, role, and state when the component is not a native semantic control.
accessibility.contrast: Text, icons, borders, and state indicators must meet WCAG contrast expectations for the dark theme.
accessibility.focus: Use visible focus styling based on semantic focus tokens and manage focus when opening or closing composite widgets.

implementationNotes: Use semantic token references only; do not hard-code visual values for requirement link.
Phase 2 reserves the ID and expected role; exact behavior, variants, and examples require later project validation.
validation: Validate requirement link for token usage, accessible names where applicable, dark-theme contrast, and visible focus states.
Treat as not fully standardized until Phase 3 or later prose standards define detailed behavior.

components.name: Run Test Result Summary
components.description: compact pass, fail, warning, not-run, and coverage summary for execution results.
useWhen: Use run test result summary when the interface needs compact pass, fail, warning, not-run, and coverage summary for execution results.
avoidWhen: Avoid run test result summary when a simpler native element or existing component communicates the same intent with less complexity.
